L. 110–246, § 4(a)122 Stat. 1664May 22, 2008June 18, 2008“(a).—EstablishmentNot later than 18 months after the date of the enactment of this Act [], the Secretary of Agriculture shall establish an advisory committee, to be known as the ‘Advisory Committee on Minority Farmers’ (in this section referred to as the ‘Committee’).June 18, 2008“(b).—DutiesThe Committee shall provide advice to the Secretary on—“(1)the implementation of section 2501 of the Food, Agriculture, Conservation, and Trade Act of 1990 ();7 U.S.C. 2279“(2)methods of maximizing the participation of minority farmers and ranchers in Department of Agriculture programs; and“(3)civil rights activities within the Department as such activities relate to participants in such programs.“(c)Membership.—“(1).—In generalThe Committee shall be composed of not more than 15 members, who shall be appointed by the Secretary, and shall include—“(A)not less than four socially disadvantaged farmers or ranchers (as defined in section 2501(e)(2) [now 2501(a)(2)] of the Food, Agriculture, Conservation, and Trade Act of 1990 () [now ]);7 U.S.C. 2279(e)(2)7 U.S.C. 2279(a)(5)“(B)not less than two representatives of nonprofit organizations with a history of working with minority farmers and ranchers;“(C)not less than two civil rights professionals;“(D)not less than two representatives of institutions of higher education with demonstrated experience working with minority farmers and ranchers; and“(E)such other persons as the Secretary considers appropriate.“(2).—Ex-officio membersThe Secretary may appoint such employees of the Department of Agriculture as the Secretary considers appropriate to serve as ex-officio members of the Committee.”[ and enacted identical provisions. was repealed by , set out as a note under .]Pub. A, § 101(a) [title VII, § 741]112 Stat. 2681Oct. 21, 1998“(a)To the extent permitted by the Constitution, any civil action to obtain relief with respect to the discrimination alleged in an eligible complaint, if commenced not later than 2 years after the date of the enactment of this Act [], shall not be barred by any statute of limitations.Oct. 21, 1998“(b)The complainant may, in lieu of filing a civil action, seek a determination on the merits of the eligible complaint by the Department of Agriculture if such complaint was filed not later than 2 years after the date of enactment of this Act []. The Department of Agriculture shall—Oct. 21, 1998“(1)provide the complainant an opportunity for a hearing on the record before making that determination;“(2)award the complainant such relief as would be afforded under the applicable statute from which the eligible complaint arose notwithstanding any statute of limitations; and“(3)to the maximum extent practicable within 180 days after the date a determination of an eligible complaint is sought under this subsection conduct an investigation, issue a written determination and propose a resolution in accordance with this subsection.“(c)Notwithstanding subsections (a) and (b), if an eligible claim is denied administratively, the claimant shall have at least 180 days to commence a cause of action in a Federal court of competent jurisdiction seeking a review of such denial.“(d)The United States Court of Federal Claims and the United States District Court shall have exclusive original jurisdiction over—“(1)any cause of action arising out of a complaint with respect to which this section waives the statute of limitations; and“(2)any civil action for judicial review of a determination in an administrative proceeding in the Department of Agriculture under this section.“(e)As used in this section, the term ‘eligible complaint’ means a nonemployment related complaint that was filed with the Department of Agriculture before and alleges discrimination at any time during the period beginning on and ending —July 1, 1997January 1, 1981December 31, 1996“(1)in violation of the Equal Credit Opportunity Act ( et seq.) in administering—15 U.S.C. 1691“(A)a farm ownership, farm operating, or emergency loan funded from the Agricultural Credit Insurance Program Account; or“(B)a housing program established under title V of the Housing Act of 1949 [ et seq.]; or42 U.S.C. 1471“(2)in the administration of a commodity program or a disaster assistance program.“(f)This section shall apply in fiscal year 1999 and thereafter.“(g)The standard of review for judicial review of an agency action with respect to an eligible complaint is de novo review. Chapter 5 of title 5 of the United States Code shall apply with respect to an agency action under this section with respect to an eligible complaint, without regard to section 554(a)(1) of that title.”

L. 110–246, § 4(a)122 Stat. 1664May 22, 2008June 18, 2008“(a).—EstablishmentNot later than 18 months after the date of the enactment of this Act [], the Secretary of Agriculture shall establish an advisory committee, to be known as the ‘Advisory Committee on Minority Farmers’ (in this section referred to as the ‘Committee’).June 18, 2008“(b).—DutiesThe Committee shall provide advice to the Secretary on—“(1)the implementation of section 2501 of the Food, Agriculture, Conservation, and Trade Act of 1990 ();7 U.S.C. 2279“(2)methods of maximizing the participation of minority farmers and ranchers in Department of Agriculture programs; and“(3)civil rights activities within the Department as such activities relate to participants in such programs.“(c)Membership.—“(1).—In generalThe Committee shall be composed of not more than 15 members, who shall be appointed by the Secretary, and shall include—“(A)not less than four socially disadvantaged farmers or ranchers (as defined in section 2501(e)(2) [now 2501(a)(2)] of the Food, Agriculture, Conservation, and Trade Act of 1990 () [now ]);7 U.S.C. 2279(e)(2)7 U.S.C. 2279(a)(5)“(B)not less than two representatives of nonprofit organizations with a history of working with minority farmers and ranchers;“(C)not less than two civil rights professionals;“(D)not less than two representatives of institutions of higher education with demonstrated experience working with minority farmers and ranchers; and“(E)such other persons as the Secretary considers appropriate.“(2).—Ex-officio membersThe Secretary may appoint such employees of the Department of Agriculture as the Secretary considers appropriate to serve as ex-officio members of the Committee.”[ and enacted identical provisions. was repealed by , set out as a note under .]Pub. The Department of Agriculture shall—Oct. 21, 1998“(1)provide the complainant an opportunity for a hearing on the record before making that determination;“(2)award the complainant such relief as would be afforded under the applicable statute from which the eligible complaint arose notwithstanding any statute of limitations; and“(3)to the maximum extent practicable within 180 days after the date a determination of an eligible complaint is sought under this subsection conduct an investigation, issue a written determination and propose a resolution in accordance with this subsection.“(c)Notwithstanding subsections (a) and (b), if an eligible claim is denied administratively, the claimant shall have at least 180 days to commence a cause of action in a Federal court of competent jurisdiction seeking a review of such denial.“(d)The United States Court of Federal Claims and the United States District Court shall have exclusive original jurisdiction over—“(1)any cause of action arising out of a complaint with respect to which this section waives the statute of limitations; children.text: The term “Department” means the Department of Agriculture.

children.heading: Socially disadvantaged farmer or rancher
children.label: 5 Socially disadvantaged farmer or rancher
children.text: The term “socially disadvantaged farmer or rancher” means a farmer or rancher who is a member of a socially disadvantaged group.

children.heading: Socially disadvantaged group
children.label: 6 Socially disadvantaged group
children.text: The term “socially disadvantaged group” means a group whose members have been subjected to racial or ethnic prejudice because of their identity as members of a group without regard to their individual qualities.

children.heading: Outreach and technical assistance
children.label: A Outreach and technical assistance
children.text: The Secretary may make grants to, and enter into contracts and other agreements with, an eligible entity that has demonstrated an ability to carry out the requirements described in paragraph (3) to provide outreach and technical assistance to socially disadvantaged farmers and ranchers and veteran farmers and ranchers under this subsection.

children.label: B Relationship to other law
children.text: The authority to carry out this section shall be in addition to any other authority provided in this or any other Act.

children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (2), the Secretary may make grants to, and enter into contracts and other agreements with, an organization or institution that received funding under this section before , to carry out a project that is similar to a project for which the organization or institution received such funding.January 1, 1996

i

children.text: in an amount that is not more than $250,000 for each year of the grant, contract, or agreement.

children.heading: Priority
children.label: F Priority
children.text: In making grants and entering into contracts and other agreements under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall give priority to nongovernmental and community-based organizations with an expertise in working with socially disadvantaged farmers and ranchers or veteran farmers and ranchers.

children.heading: Regional balance
children.label: G Regional balance
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, the Secretary shall ensure the geographical diversity of eligible entities to which grants are made and contracts and other agreements are entered into under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: H Prohibition
children.text: A grant, contract, or other agreement under subparagraph (A) may not be used for the planning, repair, rehabilitation, acquisition, or construction of a building or facility.

children.heading: Input from eligible entities
children.label: J Input from eligible entities
children.text: The Secretary shall seek input from eligible entities providing technical assistance under this subsection not less than once each year to ensure that the program is responsive to the eligible entities providing that technical assistance.

children.text: The Secretary shall award a grant to a college or university eligible to receive funds under the Act of ( et seq.), including Tuskegee University, to establish a policy research center to be known as the “Socially Disadvantaged Farmers and Ranchers Policy Research Center” for the purpose of developing policy recommendations for the protection and promotion of the interests of socially disadvantaged farmers and ranchers.August 30, 18907 U.S.C. 321

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: To be eligible to receive a grant or enter into a cooperative agreement under this subsection, the recipient of the grant or participant in the cooperative agreement shall be a collaborative State, Tribal, local, or regionally-based network or partnership of public or private entities.

children.label: i
children.text: a State cooperative extension service;

children.label: ii
children.text: a Federal, State, municipal, or Tribal agency;

children.label: iii
children.text: a community-based or nongovernmental organization;

children.label: iv
children.text: a college or university (including an institution awarding an associate’s degree) or foundation maintained by a college or university; or

children.label: v
children.text: any other appropriate partner, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Terms of grants or cooperative agreement
children.label: 4 Terms of grants or cooperative agreement
A
children.text: be for a term of not longer than 3 years; and

children.label: B
children.text: provide not more than $250,000 for each year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), to be eligible to receive a grant or enter into a cooperative agreement under this subsection, a recipient or participant shall provide a match in the form of cash or in-kind contributions in an amount equal to 25 percent of the funds provided by the grant or cooperative agreement.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: The Secretary may waive or reduce the matching requirement in subparagraph (A) if the Secretary determines such a waiver or modification is necessary to effectively reach an underserved area or population.

children.heading: Regional balance
children.label: 7 Regional balance
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, the Secretary shall ensure the geographical diversity of recipients of grants or participants in cooperative agreements under this subsection.

children.heading: Priority
children.label: 8 Priority
children.text: In making grants or entering into cooperative agreements under this subsection, the Secretary shall give priority to partnerships and collaborations that are led by or include nongovernmental, community-based organizations and school-based educational organizations with expertise in new agricultural producer training and outreach.

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: 9 Prohibition
children.text: A grant made or cooperative agreement entered into under this subsection may not be used for the planning, repair, rehabilitation, acquisition, or construction of a building or facility.

children.heading: Coordination permitted
children.label: 10 Coordination permitted
children.text: A recipient of a grant or participant in a cooperative agreement under this subsection may coordinate with a recipient of a grant or cooperative agreement under in addressing the needs of veteran farmers and ranchers with disabilities.section 5933 of this title

children.heading: Consecutive awards
children.label: 11 Consecutive awards
children.text: A grant or cooperative agreement under this subsection may be made to a recipient or participant for consecutive years.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a fair and efficient external peer review process, which the Secretary shall use in making grants or entering into cooperative agreements under this subsection.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: B Requirement
children.text: The peer review process under subparagraph (A) shall include a review panel composed of a broad representation of peers of the applicant for the grant or cooperative agreement that are not applying for a grant or cooperative agreement under this subsection.

children.heading: Participation by other farmers and ranchers
children.label: 13 Participation by other farmers and ranchers
A
children.text: is appropriate; and

children.label: B
children.text: will not detract from the primary purpose of increasing opportunities for beginning farmers and ranchers.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish beginning farmer and rancher education teams to develop curricula, conduct educational programs and workshops for beginning farmers and ranchers in diverse geographical areas of the United States, or provide training and technical assistance initiatives for beginning farmers or ranchers or for trainers and service providers that work with beginning farmers or ranchers.

children.heading: Curriculum
children.label: B Curriculum
children.text: In promoting the development of curricula, educational programs and workshops, or training and technical assistance initiatives under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall, to the maximum extent practicable, include content tailored to specific audiences of beginning farmers and ranchers, based on crop diversity or regional diversity.

children.label: i
children.text: obtain the short-term services of specialists with knowledge and expertise in programs serving beginning farmers and ranchers; and

children.label: ii
children.text: use officers and employees of the Department with direct experience in programs of the Department that may be taught as part of the curriculum for the program or workshop.

children.label: III

children.label: IV
children.text: colleges and universities (including an institution awarding an associate’s degree) or foundations maintained by a college or university; and

children.label: V
children.text: other appropriate partners, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Cooperative agreements
children.label: ii Cooperative agreements
children.text: The Secretary may enter into a cooperative agreement to reflect the terms of any cooperation under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Curriculum and training clearinghouse
children.label: 15 Curriculum and training clearinghouse
children.text: The Secretary shall establish an online clearinghouse that makes available to beginning farmers and ranchers education curricula and training materials and programs, which may include online courses for direct use by beginning farmers and ranchers.

children.heading: Application requirements
children.label: e Application requirements
children.text: In making grants and entering into contracts and other agreements, as applicable, under subsections (c) and (d), the Secretary shall make available a simplified application process for an application for a grant that requests less than $50,000.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall designate from existing Federal personnel resources in the county or region a qualified person who shall, in cooperation with the State cooperative extension services, implement the policies and programs established or modified in accordance with this section.

children.heading: Additional personnel
children.label: 2 Additional personnel
children.text: In counties or regions in which the number of socially disadvantaged farmers and ranchers or veteran farmers and ranchers exceeds 25 percent of the total number of farmers and ranchers in the county or region, the Secretary shall designate additional personnel to implement the policies and programs established or modified in accordance with this section.

children.heading: Affirmative action, appeals, and contracting review
children.label: h Affirmative action, appeals, and contracting review

children.heading: Purpose
children.label: 1 Purpose
children.text: It is the purpose of this subsection to direct the Secretary to analyze within the Department the design and implementation of affirmative action programs and policies, the appeals process for complaints of discrimination, and contracting and purchasing practices employed by the Department.

children.label: A
children.text: an assessment of the successes and failures of these affirmative action programs and policies;

children.label: B
children.text: a review of the reasons for the successes and failures described in subparagraph (A);

children.label: C
children.text: a review of procurement, contracting, and purchasing policies of the Department, the level of participation of socially disadvantaged businesses in such activities, and the impact of those policies on the participation of members of socially disadvantaged groups in such contracting with the Department;

children.label: D
children.text: a review of the reasons for participation or lack of participation of businesses owned by members of socially disadvantaged groups in the activities described in subparagraph (C); and

children.label: i
children.text: the number of complaints or allegations regarding acts, practices, or patterns of discrimination;

children.label: ii
children.text: the manner in which the complaints were investigated and resolved by the Department; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the longest, shortest, and average periods of time taken to investigate and resolve the complaints or allegations regarding acts, practices, or patterns of discrimination.

children.heading: Report
children.label: 3 Report
children.text: Not later than , and not later than , the Secretary shall prepare and submit to the Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate a report containing the information described in paragraph (2).November 28, 1991March 1, 2020

children.heading: Consolidated suboffice
children.label: 1 Consolidated suboffice
children.text: The Secretary shall require the Farm Service Agency and Natural Resources Conservation Service, and such other offices and functions the Secretary may choose to include where there has been a need demonstrated, in each county that has a reservation within its borders, to establish a consolidated suboffice at the tribal headquarters of said reservation and to staff said suboffice as needed, using existing staff, but no less than one day a week or under such other arrangement agreed to by the tribe and the Department offices.

children.heading: Cooperative agreements
children.label: 2 Cooperative agreements
children.text: For those reservations that are located in more than one county, the Secretary, the relevant county offices and the tribe shall enter into a cooperative agreement to provide the services required by paragraph (1) that avoids duplication of effort.

children.heading: Accurate documentation
children.label: j Accurate documentation
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure, to the maximum extent practicable, that the Census of Agriculture and studies carried out by the Economic Research Service accurately document the number, location, and economic contributions of socially disadvantaged farmers or ranchers in agricultural production.

children.heading: Veteran farmers and ranchers
children.label: B Veteran farmers and ranchers
children.text: Not less than 5 percent of the amounts made available to carry out subsection (d) for a fiscal year shall be used to support programs and services that address the needs of veteran farmers and ranchers.

children.heading: Interagency funding
children.label: 5 Interagency funding
children.text: Any agency of the Department may participate in any grant, contract, or agreement entered into under this section by contributing funds, if the contributing agency determines that the objectives of the grant, contract, or agreement will further the authorized programs of the contributing agency.

children.heading: Administrative expenses
children.label: 6 Administrative expenses
children.text: Not more than 5 percent of the amounts made available to carry out this section for a fiscal year may be used for expenses relating to the administration of this section.

children.heading: Limitation on indirect costs
children.label: 7 Limitation on indirect costs
children.text: A recipient of a grant or a party to a contract or other agreement under subsection (c) or (d) may not use more than 10 percent of the funds received for the indirect costs of carrying out a grant, contract, or other agreement.
